category_title: Students who use ostentation and masking to balance work and play
minimal_context: The student is practicing somersaults and rolls on mats in a tumbling workshop while the teacher divides attention among different groups.
dialogue:
TUTOR: For this tumbling workshop, please practice your somersaults and rolls on the mats.

STUDENT: (Performs a somersault) Teacher, look! Is it good? I want to be sure I'm doing it right!

TUTOR: Keep practicing. I am going to check on the other group of students now.

STUDENT: (Quickly shoves Ines just when she does the exercise so she falls, and laughs with friends) Watch this, I'm going to do the most spectacular jump!

TUTOR: Make sure you are focusing on the required exercises.

STUDENT: (Takes a long run before jumping, does the required somersault without getting up afterwards, stays lying down on his back, then lightly slaps Alice who is about to do a roll) Teacher, come here! I need help for the stall turn! See, I'm working!

options.A: Move to the mat, move the student into a sitting position, kneel by their feet, and grab their hands.
options.B: Allow the student to withdraw from the tumbling workshop and step away from the mats.
options.C: Facilitate an open discussion with the group about these challenging behaviors during the tumbling workshop.
options.D: Help and congratulate a peer who is trying to do the exercise, and do not pay attention to the student who is just playing.
